market_overview.headline: Markets staged a relief rally on Iran war hopes but remain structurally weak with both SPY and QQQ in bearish EMA structure.
market_overview.detail: Only 25.7% of stocks in Full Bull vs 37% in Full Bear, while both major indices show bearish unstacked EMAs despite today's +1% bounce. BTC remains deeply bearish at -12.8% vs 21W EMA, signaling continued risk-off conditions.

momentum_themes.headline: Energy and Defense Leading Momentum
momentum_themes.detail: Energy minerals dominate with 100% bull ratio driven by Iran war premium, while defense and semiconductor stocks show accelerating momentum. Storage/networking plays like LITE and CIEN leading tech momentum.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominates, Financials Under Pressure

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score driven by Iran war supply disruption fears
Infrastructure and defense services benefiting from geopolitical tensions
Industrial equipment and aerospace showing resilience

weakest.note: Business services completely bearish amid economic uncertainty
Software and cloud names under heavy pressure despite AI narrative
Banks and insurers suffering from rate cut fears and credit concerns

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from growth/tech into defensive energy and industrial plays. Financials remain under pressure despite some reversal candidates emerging.

items.text: BTC deeply bearish structure (-12.8% vs 21W) suggests continued risk-off environment
37% of stocks in Full Bear vs only 25.7% in Full Bull — structural weakness persists
Relief rally may be short-lived if Iran tensions don't resolve quickly
Technology services sector 82.4% bearish despite crossover alerts in mega-caps

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets rallied on hopes for Iran conflict resolution as Trump extends ultimatum and potential diplomatic solutions emerge. Oil prices surged on supply disruption fears while defense stocks benefited from escalating tensions. Energy sector leadership reflects market focus on geopolitical risk premium.

items.text: Iran war developments dominate with Trump extending ultimatum and diplomatic discussions
Oil rebounds sharply on supply risk fears and refinery disruptions
Defense contractors rally on increased military spending expectations
Energy sector sees 100% bullish participation amid supply disruption concerns
Relief rally questioned by analysts as structural market issues persist
